rxn_class_summary: Asymmetric electrochemical cyclopropanation of α,β-unsaturated aldehydes with malonates via iminium-ion organocatalysis and anodically generated electrophilic iodine species (Michael-initiated ring closure)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (Et4NI) to electrophilic iodine species (I+/I2), which iodinates the enamine intermediate; cathode reduces protons (from HFIP) to H2 as the counter half-reaction

key_intermediates.name: iminium ion from condensation of diarylprolinol-OTMS catalyst I with cinnamaldehyde (i)
enamine after Michael addition of dimethyl malonate to iminium (ii)
Michael adduct aldehyde 4a (hydrolysis product of enamine ii)
α-iodo iminium ion (iii) from iodination of enamine by electrophilic I+
cyclopropane-bound iminium ion (iv) after intramolecular SN2 ring closure
electrophilic iodine species (I+ / I2) generated at anode

electron_flow: Organocatalyst condenses with enal to form an iminium ion that accepts malonate (Michael addition) giving an enamine; the anode oxidizes iodide to electrophilic iodine, which α-iodinates the enamine to an α-iodo iminium; intramolecular enolate displacement of iodide closes the cyclopropane and hydrolysis releases the product and regenerates catalyst, while H2 evolves at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only minor suppression of 3a (36% NMR yield); TEMPO-adduct 6 isolated (25%); result deemed ambiguous because TEMPO can be oxidized to N-oxoammonium and react with enamine in polar fashion

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclopropane 3a still formed in 38% yield with only 4% of 4a, indicating no quenching of putative enamine radical cation — supports a polar pathway

probe_reagents.name: pre-synthesized intermediate 4a (stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: Under standard electrochemical conditions 4a gave 43% 3a (and 17% 5a); without catalyst still gave 23% 3a — confirms 4a/enamine is on-cycle and ring closure is electrochemically driven

probe_reagents.name: I2 / NIS (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: No conversion of starting materials and catalyst I decomposed — controlled electrocatalytic generation of I+ is essential to protect catalyst from Grob-type fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of components
probe_reagents.observation: Iodide oxidizes at +0.19/+0.38 V; enamine derived from 4a+I oxidizes at +0.27 V (between I- peaks); other components have higher potentials — iodide is selectively oxidized first

probe_reagents.name: no-electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: Only Michael adduct 4a formed (49%), no cyclopropane — electricity required for ring closure

notes: Authors favor a fully polar mechanism (iminium/enamine + electrophilic I+ ionic alkylation), supported by BHT insensitivity, CV showing iodide as the lowest-potential oxidizable species, and ring closure from isolated 4a. An alternative SET pathway via enamine radical cation (oxidation at +0.27 V) is considered possible but disfavored. Side pathways: base-induced retro-Michael of iminium iv gives byproduct 5a; free catalyst can decompose via Grob-type fragmentation when excess electrophilic iodine is present, hence the need for catalytic, electrochemically controlled I+ generation. The reaction comprises two coupled cycles (organocatalytic, temperature-controlled; electrocatalytic, current-controlled) whose rates must be balanced.
